sourceMovie.title: Rosemary's Baby
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Rosemary's Baby works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: mother!
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Rosemary's Baby clicked for you, mother! (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Eyes Wide Shut
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Rosemary's Baby clicked for you, Eyes Wide Shut (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Rosemary's Baby?
faq.answer: Try mother!, Eyes Wide Shut, The Sum of All Fears—each shares DNA with Rosemary's Baby while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Rosemary's Baby?
faq.answer: Rosemary's Baby (1968) is best described as Drama, Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Rosemary's Baby part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Rosemary's Baby Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
